task prompt (10060 chars)
QUESTION: What is the surname of the driver with the best lap time in race number 19 in the second qualifying period?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  race number refers to raceId; second qualifying period refers to q2; best lap time refers to MIN(q2);

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the full name of the driver that has done the most shipments in 2017?
  Hint: driver refers to driver_id; full name refers to first_name, last_name; in 2017 refers to Cast(ship_date AS DATE) = 2017; Most shipment refers to Max(Sum(weight))
  SQL: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM shipment AS T1 INNER JOIN driver AS T2 ON T1.driver_id = T2.driver_id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T1.ship_date) = '2017' GROUP BY T2.first_name, T2.last_name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1

  Q: For each donation not from a teacher, state the donor account id and calculate the percentage of donation given for optional support.
  Hint: not from a teacher refers to is_teacher_acct = 'f'; donor account id refers to donor_acctid; percentage of donation = divide(donation_optional_support, donation_total)*100%

task prompt (9525 chars)
QUESTION: What are the valid e-mail addresses of the administrator of the school located in the San Bernardino county, City of San Bernardino City Unified that opened between 1/1/2009 to 12/31/2010 whose school types are public Intermediate/Middle Schools and Unified Schools?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Intermediate/Middle Schools refers to SOC = 62; Unified School refers to DOC = 54; years between 2009 and 2010 can refer to 'between 1/1/2009 to 12/31/2010'

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  SQL: SELECT COUNT(stateid) FROM state_sector_details WHERE state = 'California' AND level = '2-year' AND control = 'Public'

task prompt (9640 chars)
QUESTION: For all the drivers who finished the game in race No. 872, who is the youngest?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  race number refers to raceId; drivers who finished the race refers to time has value; the youngest is a driver where MAX(dob);

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: Who is the youngest player to have won the Purple Cap?
  Hint: Who refers to Player_Name; youngest player to have won the Purple Cap refers to min(subtract(Season_Year, DOB))
  SQL: SELECT T1.Player_Name FROM Player AS T1 INNER JOIN Season AS T2 ON T1.Player_Id = T2.Purple_Cap ORDER BY T2.Season_Year - SUBSTR(T1.DOB, 1, 4) LIMIT 1

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: State the origin country of the fastest car in the database.
  Hint: origin country refers to country; the fastest refers to max(horsepower)
  SQL: SELECT T3.country FROM data AS T1 INNER JOIN production AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID INNER JOIN country AS T3 ON T3.origin = T2.country ORDER BY T1.horsepower DESC LIMIT 1

task prompt (9608 chars)
QUESTION: Calculate the percentage whereby Hamilton was not at the 1st track of the the f1 circuit since 2010.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  percentage = DIVIDE(COUNT(raceId) where surname = 'Hamilton' and position>1), (COUNT(raceId) where surname = 'Hamilton'); since 2010 refers to year >= 2010

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What percentage of matches played on 2005/07/30 belong to the F1 division?
  Hint: Division refers to Div; DIVIDE(COUNT(Div = 'F1', Date = '2005/07/30'), COUNT(Div, Date = '2005/07/30')) as percentage;
  SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN Div = 'F1' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(Div) FROM matchs WHERE Date = '2005-07-30'

task prompt (10008 chars)
QUESTION: Name the driver with the most winning. Mention his nationality and what is his maximum point scores.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Full name of the driver refers to drivers.forename and drivers.surname; the most winning refers to MAX(COUNT(wins)); average point scores refers to MAX(points);

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: In 1998, How many wins were made by team 'CAR' per game played? Who contributed the most goals? State the player ID.
  Hint: year = 1998; wins per game played = DIVIDE(W, G); CAR refers to tmID; contributed the most goals refers to MAX(G);
  SQL: SELECT CAST(T1.W AS REAL) / T1.G, T2.playerID FROM Teams AS T1 INNER JOIN Scoring AS T2 ON T1.tmID = T2.tmID AND T1.year = T2.year WHERE T1.tmID = 'CAR' AND T1.year = 1998 GROUP BY T1.W / T1.G, T2.playerID ORDER BY SUM(T2.G) DESC LIMIT 1

task prompt (9807 chars)
QUESTION: Which race was Alex Yoong in when he was in track number less than 20?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Alex Yoong refers to the full name of the driver; Full name of the driver refers to drivers.forename and drivers.surname;track number less than 10 refers to position < 20

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How many Men's 200 Metres Freestyle events did Ian James Thorpe compete in?
  Hint: Men's 200 Metres Freestyle events refer to event_name = 'Swimming Men''s 200 metres Freestyle'; events compete in refers to event_id;
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.id) FROM person AS T1 INNER JOIN games_competitor AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.person_id INNER JOIN competitor_event AS T3 ON T2.id = T3.competitor_id INNER JOIN event AS T4 ON T3.event_id = T4.id WHERE T1.full_name = 'Ian James Thorpe' AND T4.event_name LIKE 'Swimming Men%s 200 metres Freestyle'

task prompt (9765 chars)
QUESTION: Among all European Grand Prix races, what is the percentage of the races were hosted in Germany?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  European Grand Prix races refers to races.name = 'European Grand Prix';percentage = divide(COUNT(races where country = Germany and name = 'Europearn Grand Prix'),COUNT(races where name = 'Europearn Grand Prix'))*100

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What percentage of matches played on 2005/07/30 belong to the F1 division?
  Hint: Division refers to Div; DIVIDE(COUNT(Div = 'F1', Date = '2005/07/30'), COUNT(Div, Date = '2005/07/30')) as percentage;
  SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN Div = 'F1' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(Div) FROM matchs WHERE Date = '2005-07-30'

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How much is the average salary of female employees in comparison to male employees?
  Hint: female refers to Gender = 'F'; male refers to Gender = 'M'; difference in the average salary of female against male employees = SUBTRACT(AVG(Rate where Gender = 'F')), (AVG(Rate where Gender = 'M')));
  SQL: SELECT AVG(T2.Rate) FROM Employee AS T1 INNER JOIN EmployeePayHistory AS T2 ON T1.BusinessEntityID = T2.BusinessEntityID WHERE T1.Gender = 'F'

task prompt (9665 chars)
QUESTION: Which driver ranked the first in the Canadian Grand Prix in 2007? Please give his reference name.

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: Please list the positions of the players who were born in Canada and have won the All-Rookie award.
  Hint: born in Canada refers to birthCountry = 'Canada'; pos = 'LW' refers to left winger; pos = 'RW' refers to right winger; pos = 'C' refers to center; pos = 'G' refers to goalie; pos = 'D' refers to defenceman; pos = 'W' refers to winger; pos = 'F' refers to forward
  SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T1.pos FROM Master AS T1 INNER JOIN AwardsPlayers AS T2 ON T1.playerID = T2.playerID WHERE T1.birthCountry = 'Canada' AND T2.award = 'All-Rookie'

task prompt (5992 chars)
QUESTION: List out the account numbers of female clients who are oldest and has lowest average salary, calculate the gap between this lowest average salary with the highest average salary?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Female means gender = 'F'; A11 refers to average salary; Gap = highest average salary - lowest average salary; If the person A's birthdate > B's birthdate, it means that person B is order than person A.

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: List the income and number of inhabitants of customers with an age greater than the 80% of average age of all customers?
  Hint: age greater than the 80% of average age refers to age > (AVG(age) * 0.8); income refers to INCOME_K; number of inhabitants refers to INHABITANTS_K;
  SQL: SELECT T2.INCOME_K, T2.INHABITANTS_K FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN Demog AS T2 ON T1.GEOID = T2.GEOID GROUP BY T2.INCOME_K, T2.INHABITANTS_K HAVING T1.age > 0.8 * AVG(T1.age)

task prompt (9845 chars)
QUESTION: Among the drivers that finished the race in the 2008 Chinese Grand Prix, how many of them have participated in Formula_1 races?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  COUNT(raceID) > 0 reveals that this driver participated in races; drivers who finished the race refers to time has value.

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How many athlete from China participate in the 2016 Summer Olympics?
  Hint: athletes from China refer to person_id where region_name = 'China'; the 2016 Summer Olympics refer to games_name = '2016 Summer';
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(T3.id) FROM games AS T1 INNER JOIN games_competitor AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.games_id INNER JOIN person AS T3 ON T2.person_id = T3.id INNER JOIN person_region AS T4 ON T3.id = T4.person_id INNER JOIN noc_region AS T5 ON T4.region_id = T5.id WHERE T1.games_name = '2016 Summer' AND T5.region_name = 'China'

task prompt (9745 chars)
QUESTION: How much faster in percentage is the champion than the driver who finished the race last in the 2008 Australian Grand Prix?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  how much faster in percentage = divide(subtract(incremental time, champion time), last_driver time) * 100; last driver finished time = incremental time + champion time; only champion's finished time is represented by 'HH:MM:SS.mmm'; finished the game refers to time is not null

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: How many matches in 2009 had win margins of less than 10?
  Hint: in 2009 refers to Match_Date like '2009%'; win margins of less than 10 refers to Win_Margin < 10;
  SQL: SELECT COUNT(Match_Id) FROM `Match` WHERE Match_Date LIKE '2009%' AND Win_Margin < 10

  - positionText (TEXT); values=same

FINAL SQL:

PRED result: None

GOLD SQL: WITH time_in_seconds AS ( SELECT T1.positionOrder, CASE WHEN T1.positionOrder = 1 THEN (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 1, 1) AS REAL) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 3, 2) AS REAL) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 6) AS REAL) ELSE CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 2) AS REAL) END AS time_seconds FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId WHERE T2.name = 'Australian Grand Prix' AND T1.time IS NOT NULL AND T2.year = 2008 ), champion_time AS ( SELECT time_seconds FROM time_in_seconds WHERE positionOrder = 1), last_driver_incremental AS ( SELECT time_seconds FROM time_in_seconds WHERE positionOrder = (SELECT MAX(positionOrder) FROM time_in_seconds) ) SELECT (CAST((SELECT time_seconds FROM last_driver_incremental) AS REAL) * 100) / (SELECT time_seconds + (SELECT time_seconds FROM last_driver_incremental) FROM champion_time)

GOLD result: [[None]]

task prompt (5871 chars)
QUESTION: List out the account numbers of clients who are youngest and have highest average salary?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  If the person A's birthdate < B's birthdate, it means that person B is younger than person A; A11 refers to average salary

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: List the income and number of inhabitants of customers with an age greater than the 80% of average age of all customers?
  Hint: age greater than the 80% of average age refers to age > (AVG(age) * 0.8); income refers to INCOME_K; number of inhabitants refers to INHABITANTS_K;
  SQL: SELECT T2.INCOME_K, T2.INHABITANTS_K FROM Customers AS T1 INNER JOIN Demog AS T2 ON T1.GEOID = T2.GEOID GROUP BY T2.INCOME_K, T2.INHABITANTS_K HAVING T1.age > 0.8 * AVG(T1.age)

task prompt (8995 chars)
QUESTION: What is the average time in seconds of champion for each year, before year 1975?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  only champion's finished time is represented by 'HH:MM:SS.mmm'; finished the game refers to time is not null; before year 1975 refers to year < 1975;

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the percentage of champions at the age of over 30?
  Hint: DIVIDE(COUNT(competitor_id where age > 30), COUNT(competitor_id))as percentage where medal_id = 1;
  SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.age > 30 THEN 1 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T2.person_id) FROM competitor_event AS T1 INNER JOIN games_competitor AS T2 ON T1.competitor_id = T2.id WHERE T1.medal_id = 1

FINAL SQL:

PRED result: None

GOLD SQL: WITH time_in_seconds AS ( SELECT T2.year, T2.raceId, T1.positionOrder, CASE WHEN T1.positionOrder = 1 THEN (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 1, 1) AS REAL) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 3, 2) AS REAL) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 6,2) AS REAL ) + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 9) AS REAL)/1000 ELSE 0 END AS time_seconds FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId WHERE T1.time IS NOT NULL ), champion_time AS ( SELECT year, raceId, time_seconds FROM time_in_seconds WHERE positionOrder = 1 ) SELECT year, AVG(time_seconds) FROM champion_time WHERE year < 1975 GROUP BY year HAVING AVG(time_seconds) IS NOT NULL

task prompt (9454 chars)
QUESTION: How many French drivers who obtain the laptime less than 02:00.00?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  lap time less than 02:00.00 refers to seconds < 120;

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What percentage of customers from France is in the automobile segment?
  Hint: DIVIDE(COUNT(c_name where c_mktsegment = 'AUTOMOBILE' and n_name = 'FRANCE'), COUNT(c_name where n_name = 'FRANCE')) as percentage;
  SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.c_mktsegment = 'AUTOMOBILE', 1, 0)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.c_name) FROM customer AS T1 INNER JOIN nation AS T2 ON T1.c_nationkey = T2.n_nationkey WHERE T2.n_name = 'FRANCE'

task prompt (8576 chars)
QUESTION: State code numbers of top 3 yougest drivers. How many Netherlandic drivers among them?
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  youngest driver refers to Max (year(dob)); Netherlandic and Dutch refer to the same country

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the full name of the driver that has done the most shipments in 2017?
  Hint: driver refers to driver_id; full name refers to first_name, last_name; in 2017 refers to Cast(ship_date AS DATE) = 2017; Most shipment refers to Max(Sum(weight))
  SQL: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM shipment AS T1 INNER JOIN driver AS T2 ON T1.driver_id = T2.driver_id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T1.ship_date) = '2017' GROUP BY T2.first_name, T2.last_name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1

task prompt (9688 chars)
QUESTION: Please state the reference name of the oldest German driver.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  oldest refers to MIN(year(dob)); reference names appear in drverRef.

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the full name of the driver who transported the first shipment of the company?
  Hint: first shipment of the company refers to Min(ship_date); full name refers to first_name, last_name
  SQL: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name FROM shipment AS T1 INNER JOIN driver AS T2 ON T1.driver_id = T2.driver_id ORDER BY T1.ship_date ASC LIMIT 1

task prompt (9268 chars)
QUESTION: Who is the champion of the Canadian Grand Prix in 2008? Indicate his finish time.
HINT (use these definitions exactly):
  Only the time of the champion shows in the format of "hour: minutes: seconds.millionsecond";

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: Who was the captain of the winning team in the match held on 1st June 2008?
  Hint: player's name refers to Player_Name; captain refers to Role_Desc = 'Captain'; the winning team refers to Match_Winner = Team_Id; on 1st June 2008 refers to Match_Date = '2008-06-01'
  SQL: SELECT T3.Player_Name FROM Player_Match AS T1 INNER JOIN Match AS T2 ON T2.Match_Id = T1.Match_Id INNER JOIN Player AS T3 ON T3.Player_Id = T1.Player_Id INNER JOIN Rolee AS T4 ON T4.Role_Id = T1.Role_Id WHERE T2.Match_Date = '2008-06-01' AND T4.Role_Desc = 'Captain' AND T2.Match_Winner = T1.Team_Id
